key_issues: I1 MUST-FIX (the only blocker, one-line): N1's invariant is firmly committed and tested (per-compile-keyed; the h1=minLength/h2=pattern two-compile-from-one-provider distinctness fixture is exactly right), BUT the spec still lists THREE co-equal encodings 'implementer picks one', and one of them — `provider-compile` returns `(values handle warnings)` — is a trap. It violates item 010's port contract (AC 123: provider-compile returns a SINGLE compiled-validator? handle; the gen: generic is single-value, and changing its arity is the 'NO new port surface' the Scope guard forbids), AND it crashes the spec's own single-value test helpers `(validate (provider-compile P schema) value)` with a result-arity-mismatch (2 values in 1-value context), AND it makes the `(warnings-of h)` abstraction unimplementable (warnings would be a second return value, not recoverable from h alone). Strike that option; commit to ONE of the two surviving handle-keyed encodings (recommend a provider-held weak (hasheq handle -> warnings) with a `provider-warnings-for` read accessor). This is the literal residue of the original N1 indecision defect, narrowed to one bad option.

RESOLVED: N1 invariant (per-compile-keyed, NOT a single provider slot; impossible compiled-validator-warnings accessor dropped; two-compile distinctness fixture added), N2 (recorded list is now the load-bearing warn-once oracle as a CONJUNCTION — list length identical after 3 validates as after 0; 'equivalently' removed), N3 (emission pinned to eprintf/current-error-port or make-log-receiver count; no vacuous log-warning capture), and S-a..S-f (C1xC4 zero-error, compile-time recording independent of verdict, malformed-deferred-value tested branch, recursive sub-schema malformation policy, located nested-enum path, empty-vs-absent properties default-guard) are all genuinely and falsifiably pinned with exact-count/membership fixtures.

SUGGESTED S-g (real trap, recommend folding in with I1): the warnings fixtures assert (member "minLength" (warnings-of h)) using STRINGS, but schema keys are SYMBOLS ('minLength) — if the impl records symbols (the natural hash-keys result), the string-based member checks fail. Pin whether (warnings-of h) returns strings or symbols and make every fixture consistent (recommend symbols: (member 'minLength …)). Same symbol/string boundary S8 nailed for required.

SUGGESTED S-h/S-i (optional): N1 fixture could add (= 1 (length (warnings-of h1))) to catch a shared-list append bug (largely covered by the existing pattern-not-in-h1 assert); and one 'validate an old handle after later compiles — still correct' assertion to confirm a provider-side warnings map doesn't perturb an old handle's verdict. Use a WEAK hasheq for the handle->warnings map (note in Decisions) to avoid the provider retaining every handle it ever compiled (leak when item 012 compiles many schemas through one long-lived provider).
